title: aciphylla intermedia
intro: Aciphylla intermedia, also known as New Zealand carrot or parsnip, is a biennial or perennial plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Aciphylla intermedia can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried root, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
